commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=61d7c779e1398c0bd47c3220e459964974bd466e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds prompt-cache boundary helpers, dynamic before_prompt_build system-context fields, embedded-runner runtime-context routing below the cache marker, SDK API baseline updates, JSDoc updates, and regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main routes volatile runtime context through appendSystemContext, and the linked issue supplies live cache-miss telemetry for that class. I did not run a live provider reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs author-provided current-head proof plus maintainer/API follow-up; ClawSweeper repair should not proceed while the real-behavior proof gate is still insufficient.

target.title: fix(prompt-cache): preserve prefix across hook/runtime context
target.body:
## Summary

- **Update (after PR-review feedback, 2026-05-22):** the previous patch routed every `prependSystemContext` / `appendSystemContext` through the cache-boundary helper, placing them BELOW the marker. That regressed the documented contract for those fields, which the public JSDoc on `PluginHookBeforePromptBuildResult` describes as cacheable static-guidance ("so providers can cache it... to avoid per-turn token cost"). Bundled plugins (`extensions/diffs`, `extensions/skill-workshop`) pass static const guidance through these fields and rely on it being cached. This PR has been redesigned to preserve that contract while still fixing the underlying cache-miss measured in #85203.
- **Problem:** Internal callers in `attempt.ts` were passing per-turn volatile runtime context through the static `appendSystemContext` slot. Because that slot is documented to land in the cacheable prefix region, the per-turn-varying runtime context bytes were invalidating the prefix cache on every turn. The cache-miss measured in #85203 (~50-65% hit vs achievable 88-94%) reflects this misuse of the static slot for volatile content, not a routing bug in the helper.
- **Solution:** separate static cacheable hook context from dynamic per-turn hook context at the type level. (1) Restore `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` to keep `prependSystemContext` / `appendSystemContext` in the cacheable prefix region (pre-#85375 flat join semantics). (2) Add two new fields `prependDynamicSystemContext` / `appendDynamicSystemContext` to `PluginHookBeforePromptBuildResult`; they route through `ensureSystemPromptCacheBoundary` + `prepend/append-after-cache-boundary` to land BELOW the marker. (3) Migrate the internal callers in `attempt.ts` that were abusing the static slot for volatile runtime context to the new dynamic fields. (4) Tighten JSDoc on existing static fields with an explicit warning against per-turn volatile content.
- **What changed (overall PR, including this redesign):** 4 commits on this branch plus an upstream merge. Commit 1 `a100ed525d` added new helpers (`ensureSystemPromptCacheBoundary`, `appendSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary`) + marker sanitization + refactored `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` (the part that was wrong) + the `appendRuntimeExtraSystemPrompt` override-branch parity fix. Commit 2 `2f5042653d` added lock-in test assertions (the parts that locked the wrong contract have been reverted in commit 4; the override-branch + helper assertions stay). Commit 3 `eee1e1a7a5` added documentation JSDoc (now corrected in commit 4). Commit 4 `b002ce4b47` is the redesign: revert wrong placement, add new dynamic fields, migrate internal callers, update tests, regenerate the plugin SDK API baseline. Commit 5 `61d7c779e1` merges upstream/main and regenerates the SDK baseline against the merged state.
- **What did NOT change:** the override-branch parity fix in `attempt-system-prompt.ts`, the new helpers, and the marker sanitization are retained from the previous patch. The override-branch change moves the per-turn volatile `extraSystemPrompt` (subagent context, group chat context) below the marker, matching the regular path's documented design at `src/agents/system-prompt.ts:1253-1254` ("Channel/session-specific guidance lives below the cache boundary so large stable workspace context can remain a byte-identical prefix across turns"). Sites 1/2/4/5/7 from #85203 (user-turn text / inbound message body) remain out of scope. Google managed cache paths are out of scope per the issue.

## Motivation

The cache-boundary marker keeps the system-prompt stable prefix byte-identical across turns so providers can hit prompt cache from turn 2 onward. The original V3 patch tried to fix #85203 by routing all hook-supplied system context below the marker, but that regressed cacheability for bundled plugins that legitimately use the static-guidance fields. The correct shape is to give per-turn volatile system content its own field (with documented below-marker routing) and leave the static-guidance fields where they have always been documented to live.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature (new optional public hook fields: `prependDynamicSystemContext`, `appendDynamicSystemContext`)
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs (JSDoc tightening on existing static fields)
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts (additive new fields on the plugin hook result type)
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #85203
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

Not auto-closed: the issue body lists 6 dist:line sites; this PR ships the system-prompt paths and the documented-vs-actual contract reconciliation. Sites 1/2/4/5/7 operate on user-turn text or inbound message body and are out of scope. Maintainers may close #85203 after merge if the redesign is accepted, or keep it open to track any of the unaddressed surfaces.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:

The bug class is "per-turn volatile content invalidating the cacheable prefix". The original patch tried to fix it by moving every `*SystemContext` field below the marker, but that broke the documented contract for bundled-plugin static-guidance callers. The redesigned fix: (a) keep the existing static fields where the JSDoc says they live, (b) add new explicitly-volatile fields routed below the marker, (c) migrate the internal abuse callers (per-turn runtime context that was using the static slot) to the new dynamic fields. The routing-below-marker semantics in this PR are exactly the routing-below-marker semantics validated by the original 3-turn live-provider proof; only the public field name on the calling code changed (volatile content now flows through `appendDynamicSystemContext` instead of `appendSystemContext`).

- Real environment tested:

Standalone Node ESM proof script (`/tmp/proof-cache.mjs`) imports `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` and the boundary helpers from BOTH the build of this branch and the build of `upstream/main 6bd430ee35`, sends 3-turn sequences with varying hook content to live provider APIs, and captures cache token counters from real responses. The script reuses the production `applyAnthropicCacheControlToSystem` block-split logic (`splitSystemPromptCacheBoundary` â†’ two-block system with `cache_control` on the stable-prefix block only) so the Anthropic payload shape is identical to what OpenClaw emits at runtime.

- Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```
pnpm -F openclaw build         # this branch (post-redesign)
pnpm -F openclaw build         # baseline worktree at upstream/main 6bd430ee35
node /tmp/proof-cache.mjs      # imports both dists, hits both providers, prints results
```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture, copied live output):

Stable-prefix byte equality across the 3 turns (extracted via `splitSystemPromptCacheBoundary` on the composed system prompt):

```
baseline stable_prefix_byte_identical_across_turns = false
baseline stable_prefix_lengths = [22843, 22840, 22841]

V3       stable_prefix_byte_identical_across_turns = true
V3       stable_prefix_lengths = [22736, 22736, 22736]
```

Anthropic `/v1/messages` response usage (cache-eligible Anthropic model, 5-minute ephemeral cache):

```
                  baseline                              V3
turn 1   cache_creation=5045  cache_read=0    cache_creation=5006  cache_read=0
turn 2   cache_creation=5045  cache_read=0    cache_creation=0     cache_read=5006
turn 3   cache_creation=5045  cache_read=0    cache_creation=0     cache_read=5006
```

OpenAI `/v1/chat/completions` response usage (cache-eligible OpenAI model, auto prefix cache):

```
                  baseline                              V3
turn 1   prompt=4840  cached=0               prompt=4840  cached=0
turn 2   prompt=4844  cached=0               prompt=4844  cached=4736
turn 3   prompt=4844  cached=0               prompt=4844  cached=4736
```

The terminal output above was captured against commit `a100ed525d` of this branch (the original V3 patch, pre-redesign). The redesigned commit `b002ce4b47` preserves the routing-below-marker semantics for the volatile content path; only the calling-code field name changed (`appendSystemContext` â†’ `appendDynamicSystemContext` on the internal abuse callers). The byte-stable prefix property, the helper composition, and the per-provider cache behavior carry over unchanged for the dynamic path.

- Observed result after fix:

Baseline re-creates the cache on every turn (`cache_creation` on every turn, `cache_read=0` on every turn) on Anthropic and reports `cached_tokens=0` on all 3 turns on OpenAI. After the fix the cache is written on turn 1 and the full stable prefix is read from cache on turns 2 and 3 (Anthropic `cache_read=5006`, ~99% of input; OpenAI `cached_tokens=4736`, ~98% of prompt). The stable prefix is byte-identical across all 3 turns and only the dynamic suffix (the new `appendDynamicSystemContext` content + dynamic base context) varies.

- What was not tested:
    - Re-captured live-provider numbers against the redesigned commit `b002ce4b47` â€” the routing semantics for the dynamic path are unchanged; the original capture above carries over. Happy to refresh against the new commit on request.
    - Subagent override branch (`attempt-system-prompt.ts`) cache behavior against live providers â€” covered by the assertions in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-system-prompt.test.ts` that lock in the new byte sequence and marker-aware composition; behavior is the same routing through the same helper.
    - Google managed cache paths (out of scope per the issue body â€” those providers use server-side conversation cache, not prompt-prefix cache).
    - Long-TTL (`extended-cache-ttl-2025-04-11`) â€” the proof used the default 5-minute ephemeral TTL because the 3 turns fit comfortably in that window.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: the original patch misread the contract of `prependSystemContext` / `appendSystemContext`. The public JSDoc describes them as cacheable static-guidance fields and bundled plugins use them per that contract; the original patch placed them below the marker, regressing the documented case. The cache-miss in #85203 was driven by INTERNAL callers in `attempt.ts` passing per-turn volatile runtime context through the static slot â€” that abuse is what produced the measured low hit rate. The redesigned fix targets the actual abuse pattern (migrate internal callers to new dynamic fields) without breaking the documented static case.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the original proof script tested only the abuse pattern (per-turn-varying content) and never exercised the documented static-guidance case. Use-case coverage for the proof and the assertions in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts > composeSystemPromptWithHookContext cache prefix stability (#85203)` has been corrected: both axes are now covered.

## What's salvaged from the original patch

The original patch had three independent components, two of which are correct:

1. **New helpers + marker sanitization** (âś“ salvaged) â€” `ensureSystemPromptCacheBoundary` + `appendSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` plus full / comment-form marker stripping on additions. Pure additions; no contract impact; useful regardless of design direction.
2. **Override-branch parity fix** (âś“ salvaged) â€” `appendRuntimeExtraSystemPrompt` in `attempt-system-prompt.ts` now routes the override-path `extraSystemPrompt` (subagent context / group chat context) below the marker, matching the regular path's documented design at `system-prompt.ts:1253-1254`. The override path was previously emitting a fully markerless prompt, so override + subagent context broke prompt cache on every subagent spawn. The internal callers of `extraSystemPrompt` (`sessions-send-tool.ts`, `sessions-send-tool.a2a.ts`, `subagent-spawn.ts`) pass per-turn volatile content; below-marker routing is the correct fit.
3. **`composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` refactor** (âś— reverted) â€” moving every `*SystemContext` field below the marker was the contract regression. Restored to pre-#85375 flat join for the static fields, with new dynamic-field routing added on top.

## AI-assisted disclosure

AI-assisted; contributor manually reviewed and is responsible for the change.
target.author: nxmxbbd
